news_items.headline: Ohtani (knee) scratched from start, to miss ASG
news_items.summary: Shohei Ohtani was scratched from his scheduled pitching start Friday due to continued irritation in his left knee

news_items.headline: Red Sox plane issues force delayed start vs. Mets
news_items.summary: The Red Sox arrived at Citi Field at 5:05 p.m. ET, a little more than two hours before the scheduled first pitch of their game against the Mets, after the team plane was grounded for nearly 24 hours due to mechanical issues.

news_items.headline: Sources: Cards' Wetherholt gets $112.5M deal
news_items.summary: Infielder JJ Wetherholt and the Cardinals are in agreement on an eight-year, $112.5 million contract extension, sources told ESPN.

news_items.headline: Phillies' Schwarber latest Home Run Derby add
news_items.summary: Phillies slugger Kyle Schwarber was added to the Home Run Derby on Friday, joining teammate Bryce Harper for Monday's event in Philadelphia.

news_items.headline: Mets' Vientos to miss 6-8 weeks with broken hand
news_items.summary: Mets infielder/designated hitter Mark Vientos will miss six to eight weeks with a fractured right hand.

news_items.headline: Reds activate Hayes, place McLain on 10-day IL
news_items.summary: Third baseman Ke'Bryan Hayes was activated off the injured list by the Reds before their game Friday night against the Cubs.

news_items.headline: Rangers' DeGrom to skip next start; IL possible
news_items.summary: Jacob deGrom will miss his next start for the Rangers because of a glute and hip strain, but the team hopes the extra time off will allow him to avoid the injured list.

news_items.headline: Breakout rookie Murakami completes HR Derby field
Game Discussion for St. Louis Cardinals vs Atlanta Braves Friday Night
news_items.summary: The St. Louis Cardinals begin the last homestand prior to the All-Star Game break with the Atlanta Braves coming to town. Grant McCray makes season debut as Bericoto placed on IL
news_items.summary: This afternoon, the San Francisco Giants announced that they placed Victor Bericoto on the 10-day IL “with an oblique strain” and taking his spot on the roster is 25-year old Grant McCray, in the majors for the first time in 2026. McCray had surgery back in June to repair his left hamate bone and up […]
